claims.text: Bill Gates designed the COVID-19 vaccines.
COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips that track people.

evidence.question: fact check: COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips that track people.
evidence.snippet: No authorized or approved vaccine contains any material related to microchips or tracking technology.

claims.text: The microchips in COVID vaccines operate via 5G networks to enable tracking.

lens_1_text_text.narrative: All three sources converge in refuting the claim. FactCheck.org (IFCN-certified) directly addresses the conspiracy theory, noting that ingredient lists for all FDA-authorized COVID-19 vaccines are publicly available and contain no microchips—the microchip narrative was fuelled by a misrepresented 2018 Pfizer CEO clip predating COVID-19 vaccines entirely. Factually.co (unknown tier, used only as corroborating detail) enumerates actual vaccine components: mRNA, lipid nanoparticles, and common stabilizers. Fact Crescendo (IFCN-certified) further explains the physical impossibility—a tracking microchip would require at least 1 mm³ and its own antenna, far exceeding vaccine-needle capacity—and confirms the FDA explicitly states no tracking devices are present. No cross-source contradictions exist; all three sources align. The claim is refuted by two independent IFCN fact-checkers citing regulatory disclosures and expert opinion.

refuted_propositions.proposition: COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips that track people.
refuted_propositions.counter_fact: Vaccine ingredients are publicly listed by the FDA; they contain only mRNA, lipids, and stabilizers—no microchips or tracking devices.

source_quality_table.rationale: No confident prior on this domain; despite the name suggesting fact-checking, it is not a recognized IFCN signatory or widely known fact-checking organization.

tone_neutral_justification: Two independent IFCN-certified fact-checkers (FactCheck.org, Fact Crescendo) and FDA public ingredient disclosures uniformly confirm no microchips are present; physical feasibility experts corroborate the finding.

presentation_payload.headline_finding: COVID-19 vaccines contain no microchips; FDA-published ingredient lists show only mRNA, lipids, and stabilizers.
presentation_payload.counter_fact: Vaccine ingredients are fully public; no microchips or tracking tech—a chip would be physically too large for a needle.
presentation_payload.load_bearing_evidence_snippet: "No authorized or approved vaccine contains any material related to microchips or tracking technology." — Fact Crescendo
